characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Accidental Hero: In the series' first episode, Dudley chases his chew toy, which winds up in the possession of D.O.O.M.. In the process of recovering his chew toy, Dudley manages to knock out Snaptrap's henchmen, and Snaptrap would have surrendered the Kruger Rat he had stolen to him had Dudley not had his mind more on his chew toy. When the Chief arrives at D.O.O.M. HQ, he is so impressed with what Dudley did that he hires him as an agent of T.U.F.F. and Kitty's partner. Ambiguously Bi: In "Freaky Spy Day", he flirts with Jack Rabbit while in Kitty's body. He eventually says heloveshim. Ass Kicks You: He sometimes attacks enemies by smacking them with his rear end. Badass Adorable: He's an endearing dog who can be a moron, but when push comes to shove, he won't be afraid to fight his enemies Basement Dweller: He still lives with his mom, at least before eventually moving to a treehouse (which isn't that much of an improvement, as said treehouse is still next door to his mother's house).

characters.name: Dudley Puppy
characters.description: Accidental Hero: In the series' first episode, Dudley chases his chew toy, which winds up in the possession of D.O.O.M.. In the process of recovering his chew toy, Dudley manages to knock out Snaptrap's henchmen, and Snaptrap would have surrendered the Kruger Rat he had stolen to him had Dudley not had his mind more on his chew toy. When the Chief arrives at D.O.O.M. HQ, he is so impressed with what Dudley did that he hires him as an agent of T.U.F.F. and Kitty's partner. Ambiguously Bi: In "Freaky Spy Day", he flirts with Jack Rabbit while in Kitty's body. He eventually says heloveshim. Ass Kicks You: He sometimes attacks enemies by smacking them with his rear end. Badass Adorable: He's an endearing dog who can be a moron, but when push comes to shove, he won't be afraid to fight his enemies Basement Dweller: He still lives with his mom, at least before eventually moving to a treehouse (which isn't that much of an improvement, as said treehouse is still next door to his mother's house).

characters.name: Kitty Katswell
characters.description: The Ace: It's safe to say that she is T.U.F.F.'s 1# agent. Action Girl: Stems from being a T.U.F.F. agent. Bonus points for how she’s arguably the ONLY female character to be an expert at combat. Actor Allusion: Her voice actress previously voiced acat named Kitty. Alliterative Name:KittyKatswell. Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: She has black hair and is by far the most composed of the main cast. Of course, because ofFlanderization, it becomes more downplayed.

characters.name: Keswick
characters.description: Alien Among Us: A minor running gag has him implied to be an alien. All Men Are Perverts: He can be like this sometimes with girls,including Tammy. Big, Thin, Short Trio: Of the three main T.U.F.F. agents, he's the Short to Dudley's Big and Kitty's Thin. Bizarre Alien Biology: He has very strange anatomy (such as being a practically different creature during certain phases of his life cycle) and under the effect of Truth Syrup reveals that he is in fact, an alien. Cartoon Creature:Word of Godsays, "[T.U.F.F.] always knows [Keswick] will have the answer, but they just don't know what kind of animal he is." Its revealed in a later episode that Keswick is actually part of his own species,also called Keswick. They have quite an unusual life cycle, including a 'platypus phase' where they shoot toxic quills when they hiccup.

characters.name: Herbert "The Chief" Dumbrowski
characters.description: Artificial Limb: The Chief has a bionic foot that makes him even cooler than he is already. Da Chief: He's the head of T.U.F.F. and issues all commands. The other cast even refer to him by his position more than his actual name. False Teeth Tomfoolery: His teeth are revealed to be dentures in "Internal Affairs". Flanderization: The Chief becomes more cowardly and immature. Four-Legged Insect: He's an insect who has four limbs and is bipedal.

characters.name: Verminious Snaptrap
characters.description: Accidental Dance Craze: In "Dog House", Snaptrap (undercover) getsscorpions in his pantsand reacts in pain that appears as if he's dancing when he's not. Basement Dweller: He is sometimes shown to be living in his mother's basement. Big Bad Wannabe: His very first appearance has him talk himself up as if he were the biggest threat in the series, only for it to turn out no one has heard of him. Overall, he's portrayed far more as anIneffectual Sympathetic Villainthan a genuine threat. Catchphrase: "It is I, Verminious Snaptrap!" and "I shall now plot myrevenge!" He also often ends conversations with "Snaptrap out!"."Start the car Larry!" whenever committing an act of petty theft."[Insert random demand here], OR PERISH!!!" "Start the car Larry!" whenever committing an act of petty theft."[Insert random demand here], OR PERISH!!!"

characters.name: Larry
characters.description: Ambiguously Jewish: He shows a lot of Jewish stereotypes. He has a big nose, balding hair, looksnerdy,is married to a Christian(Snaptrap's sister), and is always arguing or complaining with Snaptrap. Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Short to Francisco's Big and Ollie's Thin. Butt-Monkey: Snaptrap always likes to harm Larry whenever he gets mad. Even Bad Men Love Their Mamas: When the other members of D.O.O.M. complain about how they hate being ordered around by their mothers in "Mom's Away", Larry brings up that he actually loves his mother unconditionally. Unsurprisingly, this prompts Snaptrap to abuse him yet again. Half-Dressed Cartoon Animal: He wears a shirt but no pants.

characters.name: Ollie
characters.description: Affably Evil: Despite being a member of D.O.O.M., he is one of the most polite characters on the show. Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Thin to Francisco's Big and Larry's Short. Brits Love Tea: He speaks with a British accent, and is often seen holding a teacup. Cloudcuckoolander's Minder: He generally persuades his boss Snaptrap to carry out evil schemes that are actually useful rather than pointlessly selfish and/or non-threatening. Evil Brit: He's a British opossum who works for D.O.O.M..

characters.name: Francisco
characters.description: Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Big to Ollie's Thin and Larry's Short. Green Gators: He's an alligator with green scales. Half-Dressed Cartoon Animal: He wears a shirt but no pants. Never Smile at a Crocodile: He's an alligator and a D.O.O.M. agent. Top-Heavy Guy: An especially glaring example, his legs are downright puny compared to his hunched figure.

characters.name: The Chameleon
characters.description: Accidental Misnaming: His debut episode has everyone,including himselfkeep mispronouncing his name. ("It is I, the Camolee-on!") Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: Has a hard time focusing on his schemes over his stomach. Especially when ladybugs are involved. Ax-Crazy: He's a little bit...off. Especially given that some of his schemes involvegleefully blowing things/people up. Catchphrase: "It is I, The Chameleon!" Cloudcuckoolander: Even setting aside hisAx-Crazyaspects, the Chameleon is just downright odd sometimes.

characters.name: Bird Brain
characters.description: Animal Gender-Bender: He has laid eggs for comedic effect. Bald of Evil: He is a balding villain. Duck!: A running gag with many of his minions, including a duck named Duck (who is temporarily renamed Skip to avoid confusion with the command to duck, only for the rest of his minions to then mistake the utterance of his name for a command to skip). In "Bad Eggs", he shows an application form for being one of his henchmen, which includes a space for "weird name". Evil Counterpart: The fact that he's highly intelligent, something of a social outcast and is the only known specimen of his species makes him an evil counterpart to T.U.F.F.'s nerdy tech expert Keswick. Furry Baldness: He has a balding appearance in spite of his hairless scalp being blue like the rest of his feathered body.

characters.name: Zippy
characters.description: Minion with an F in Evil: Not on the same level as Owl and Bat as she still has her moments of brilliance occasionally, but she can also be just as clumsy as them when following Bird Brain's orders at times.However, this is taken to the extreme in "Bad Eggs" as she is the main reason why Bird Brain's plan doesn't go very far since she installed an eco-friendly solar powered light that prevented her boss's evil incubator from working. She even asks him if he doesn't want to save the planet when Bird Brain makes it clear that destroying it is a basic item in the mission statement. However, this is taken to the extreme in "Bad Eggs" as she is the main reason why Bird Brain's plan doesn't go very far since she installed an eco-friendly solar powered light that prevented her boss's evil incubator from working. She even asks him if he doesn't want to save the planet when Bird Brain makes it clear that destroying it is a basic item in the mission statement. Nice Girl: She is so cheerful and optimistic that most of the time her association with Bird Brain is really the only thing that qualifies her as a villainess. Even when they are in the middle of criminal acts she usually just flies around and observes while her other teammates do most of the work. The Pollyanna: Most of the time she always shows an optimistic opinion even if it is not the best of situations:After Bird Brain realizes with dismay that he's the only one of his kind present at the blue-bottomed booby convention, she says that on the bright side he'll be first in line at the buffet.Cue Owl and Bat already there getting their food.She tries to cheer up Bird Brain by stating that he is finally flying on his own thanks to the lack of gravity when the spaceship with them inside is slowly close to being consumed by the Sun in "Puppy Unplugged".Spends most of "Mom-A-Geddon" continually saying that Bird Brain can fly if he believes in himself, despite being a bird scientifically incapable of flight and always fall

characters.name: Owl and Bat
characters.description: Blind Bats: Bat's default look includes a pair ofsunglassesand the only word he seems capable of saying is "Where?". A Dog Named "Dog": Subverted with Owl, since in "Soar Loser" his brother refers to him as Terry. However it's played straight with Bat, since no one refers to him by any other name. Minion with an F in Evil: They are sometimes so ridiculously incompetent at following even the simplest orders that it's really a mystery why Bird Brain keeps having them as henchmen. One-Word Vocabulary: Owl only says "Who?" and Bat "Where?", what drives Bird Brain crazy every time they do a "Who's on First?" routine. Those Two Guys: They are never seen without each other.

characters.name: Jack Rabbit
characters.description: Barefoot Cartoon Animal: He's a cartoon rabbit who goes barefoot. Evil Former Friend: He used to be Kitty's friend and partner before becoming a criminal. Fallen Hero: He was a former T.U.F.F. agent, but he now makes a living stealing secret information. Faux Affably Evil: He gets his way by using his "charms" to get people on his side and then take advantage of them. The Name Is Bond, James Bond: As with any James Bond parody, he introduces himself by saying his last name and then his full name.

characters.name: Quacky the Duck
characters.description: Depraved Kids' Show Host: He turned to villainy after his show got canceled, and thus commits crimes while still acting out his kid-friendly persona. Early-Bird Cameo: Is mentioned and appears sporadically throughout "Kid Stuff" as a supporting character before playing an antagonistic role in his second appearance. Excited Kids' Show Host: He even continues to act like this after he gets arrested, though he's considerably calmer. Face–Heel Turn: He turns evil after his show gets cancelled by the network president. Falsely Reformed Villain: Most of his latter plans include pretending to go straight and open some kind of business.

characters.name: The Sharing Moose
characters.description: Brought to You by the Letter "S": He always wears a white shirt with a big red "M" (for moose) on the front. The Brute: He does the heavy lifting for Quacky. Cartoony Tail: He has a fairly long tail with a tassel at the end, instead of a small, stubby one like real moose have. The Dragon: He is Quacky's main helper and muscle. Half-Dressed Cartoon Animal: He doesn't wear anything besides a shirt.

characters.name: F.L.O.P.P.
characters.description: Catchphrase: Wanna-Bee has a tendency to describe things as "super bad". Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Fiddler Crab is not seen after "Top Dog". Demoted to Extra: Meerkat and Escape Goat became less prominent as Wanna-Bee began to act primarily as a standalone villain starting with the latter half of the second season. It got to the point where when the team was last seen together in "Organized Crime", Wanna-Bee seemed to have taken on the role ofthe leaderwhile Meerkat had significantly fewer lines and importance to the plot. They are alsonowhere to be seen in the third season, while he is a central character in two episodes. Even Evil Has Standards: They thought pushing all the buttons in a crowded elevator was too far. Fun with Acronyms: F.L.O.P.P. stands forFiendishLeagueOfPotentialPerpetrators.

characters.name: The Caped Cod
characters.description: Authority in Name Only: His "kingdom" is just a bunch of trash he believes are his subjects. Cloudcuckoolander: Aside from spending a lot of his time talking to literal garbage, he's under the delusion that Dudley is king of the surface world and tries to overthrow his non-existent rule. Create Your Own Villain: Downplayed. He was already a complete lunatic to begin with, but Dudley fishing and outright telling him he would become a meal led the cod to discover the existence of the surface world and eventually attempt to rule it in retaliation. Leitmotif: A similar version of the old Italian folk song "Tarantella Napoletana", in an ominous tone, can be heard in the background sometimes when he shows up. Motive Decay: His main goal in most of his appearances is to flood Petropolis and take control of the city to make it part of his non-existent kingdom. However, near the end of the series in "Cod Squad", he simply tries to end the local annual fishing tournament by using a giant mechanical shark to swallow the fishermen and then blow them up without any mention of conquering the surface world.

characters.name: Katty Katswell
characters.description: Always Someone Better: Acts as this to Kitty. While Agent Katswell is the best there is, her sister is seen as superior in every way, even preferred by their own mother more. Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking: Kitty had her arrested for armed robbery, grand theft auto, insurance fraud, and stealing her boyfriend. The Baroness: ASexy Cat Personwho's also aClassy Cat-Burglarand aFemme Fatale. Beauty Is Bad: Especially in contrast to hersister. Cain and Abel: The Cain to her goody two-shoes sister's Abel.

characters.name: The Weasel
characters.description: The Dreaded: Even Snaptrap, who is aPsychopathic Manchild, is afraid of him and was genuinely terrified when found out that he had escaped from jail seeking revenge and thathe would be his new cellmate. Evil Is Petty: Besides destroying his enemies for revenge, his only other hobby is licking other people's lollipops when he has the measles. Leitmotif: Appropriately, an ominous version of "Pop Goes The Weasel" plays whenever he makes a sudden appearance in his debut episode. Offscreen Teleportation: In his first appearance he does this a lot, including suddenly appearing in the dark in the middle of alightning stormand disappearing the same way.Snaptrap:He can pop up anywhere, he's THE WEEEEEEEEEASEL! THAT'S WHAT HE DOES!! Silent Antagonist: Acts like this for much of "Pup Goes The Weasel", until he throws it away when he comes face to face with Dudley and Snaptrap.

characters.name: The Stink Bug
characters.description: The Cameo: Is among the villains imprisoned at T.U.F.F. HQ in "D.O.O.M. and G.L.O.O.M.".May also count as anEarly-Bird Cameosince this episode was later placed before its sister episode "Law and Odor" in subsequent airings. May also count as anEarly-Bird Cameosince this episode was later placed before its sister episode "Law and Odor" in subsequent airings. Disproportionate Retribution: His plan was to use a giantstink bombcalled "Air Unfreshener" to drive everyone out of Petropolis in retaliation for his own expulsion from the city. However, according to the Chief's speech, all the citizens did was ask him to take a shower, but he made a big stink about it and left vowing revenge. Drives Like Crazy: After collecting one of the ingredients needed for his stink bomb he accidentally crashes the Stink Buggy into a sign leading to Weehawken (which is 35 miles from Atlantic City). He then says that he stinks at driving. Monster of the Week: Aside from his cameo listed above he only appears in "Law and Odor" where he acts as the main antagonist.

characters.name: Rumble Bee
characters.description: Beware My Stinger Tail: Like the other mutated bees, he has a stinger that can shoot lasers.His main plan also involves using a giant ray gun that fires stingers to turn the population into zombies. His main plan also involves using a giant ray gun that fires stingers to turn the population into zombies. Empowered Badass Normal: Implied. Wanna-Bee says he was already "super bad" before becoming a killer bee and that now he's a monster. Monster of the Week: His only appearance is in "To Bee Or Not To Bee". Red Eyes, Take Warning: As seen in the picture above he has eyes with red irises, one of them completely red and the other with a black pupil.

characters.name: Peg Puppy
characters.description: Alliterative Name: Her name and surname both start with the letter "P". Miniature Senior Citizens: Her son is an adult and she is way smaller than he is. My Beloved Smother: Dudley is frequently annoyed by his mother's nagging. Parent with New Paramour: In "Snap Dad", she dates Snaptrap, a development Dudley isn't too happy about. The episode ends with her dating the Chameleon, which elicits a similar reaction from Dudley. Species Surname: She's a dog and Dudley's mom so of course her last name is Puppy.

characters.name: Tammy
characters.description: Beauty Equals Goodness: ARighteous Rabbitworking for T.U.F.F., and is also theirSexy Secretary. A Day in the Limelight: "Organized Crime" focus all about Tammy. Genki Girl: Tammy is generally pretty cheerful, outgoing, and friendly. Graceful Ladies Like Purple: She's usually seen wearing a traditionally feminine work attire that are shades of purple. Hartman Hips: Tammy has rather wide hips which, unlike Kitty's, are more pronounced.

characters.name: Eric
characters.description: The Beautiful Elite: Not only is he a gorgeous guy, but he's secretly wealthy too. He even bought an island shaped like Kitty's head to impress her. Did Not Get the Girl: It's almost aRunning Gaghow he and Kitty miss their chances to hook up. Hidden Depths: He is actually madly in love with Kitty. He is independently wealthy and only took the job as a water delivery guy to be close to her. In one episode, Dudley (who is able to briefly tell the future) says that Kitty and Eric do get married in the future but there is a hidden secret to him that she ought to know. Neither Kitty or the audience figure out what that secret is. Hunk: Has a very attractive masculine figure, which has drawn the eye of several characters including Kitty. Mr. Fanservice: Sort of. He doesn't get manyShirtless Scenebut he is a very handsome guy.

characters.name: Mrs. Katswell
characters.description: Abusive Parent: In "Diary of a Mad Cat" we see that she makes Kitty feel inferior especially when she is compared to her criminal twin sister. Parental Issues: Kitty seems to have some serious mommy issues and as shown in "Diary of a Mad Cat", it's not hard to see why. "Well Done, Son" Guy: Despite Kitty's great accomplishments and legendary status as a noble super spy, her mother still showers love towards her criminal sister over her more heroic child. This has caused a great deal of issues with Kitty.

characters.name: Daisy
characters.description: Dumb Blond: She plays this trope to a T. She's blonde and about as much of an idiot as Dudley. Expy: Basically a female equivalent of Dudley in every way. Kindhearted Simpleton: She gets her words mixed up very easily and is very sweet, bubbly, and friendly Nice Girl: It's pretty easy to see why Dudley likes her so much.
